evals.prompt: We're a B2B SaaS for restaurant inventory management. Currently charging $99/month flat. We have customers from single-location restaurants to 20-location chains. The chains get WAY more value but pay the same as single-location. Should we change our pricing model?
evals.expected_output: Pricing model analysis recommending value-metric-based pricing (per location, per user, or per revenue managed), with specific tier recommendations and migration strategy.
assertions: Identifies the pricing problem (flat rate doesn't capture value from chains)
Recommends a value metric (per location is likely ideal here)
Addresses migration strategy for existing customers
Notes pricing psychology considerations for the transition

evals.prompt: We want to raise prices by 30% for existing customers. How do we do this without massive churn?
evals.expected_output: Price increase communication strategy with timing, messaging approach, grandfathering options, and churn mitigation tactics.
assertions: Recommends a specific communication timeline (notice period)
Covers grandfathering vs. full increase options
Provides messaging framework focused on value delivered
Addresses high-risk segment identification and proactive outreach
Notes that 30% is significant and may require segmented approach

evals.prompt: Design the layout of our pricing page
evals.expected_output: Offers pricing strategy input on tier presentation and pricing psychology, redirects to page-cro or copywriting for the actual page layout/copy.
assertions: Provides pricing strategy input (tier order, recommended plan, decoy)
Redirects to page-cro or copywriting for layout and copy
